originalTitle: Police seal off key roads in Nairobi as Kenya braces for Gen Z protests
title: Big News from Nairobi: Young People Are Speaking Up!
content:
Hello, wonderful friends, and welcome to your kid-friendly news report from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we have some important news from a super-duper city called Nairobi, which is the capital city of a beautiful country called Kenya.

What's Happening in Nairobi?

Imagine a very busy city, with tall buildings and lots of cars and people moving about. That's Nairobi! Right now, something special is happening there. Many young people are gathering together. They are holding up signs and want to share their feelings and ideas with everyone. This is called a 'protest' – it's when lots of people show that they want things to change or get better. These young people are part of a group we sometimes call 'Gen Z'. They are a bit older than you, maybe like your older brothers, sisters, or cousins. They really care about their country and want it to be a safe and fair place for everyone who lives there. They are using their voices to ask for important changes. Because so many people are gathering, the police in Nairobi are helping to keep everyone safe. They are closing off some of the big roads in the city. This might sound a bit like when your teacher asks you to stay in your classroom during a fire drill, just to make sure everyone is protected and no one gets hurt. Closing roads helps make sure there's enough space and that traffic doesn't get too tricky for the gathering. Did you know? Nairobi is a very important city in Africa! It's famous for its big national park right inside the city, where you can see amazing animals like giraffes and zebras, just a short drive from tall buildings!

Why Are People Gathering?

The young people are gathering because they are feeling sad and worried about some things that happened before. Last year, and earlier this year, there were other times when people gathered to share their feelings. Sadly, during those times, some people were hurt very badly, and sadly, some even lost their lives. This made many people very, very sad. Now, these young people, the Gen Z, are saying they want 'justice'. What does justice mean? It means making things right when something unfair or bad has happened. It's like if someone at school wasn't being fair, and you wanted to make sure everyone was treated kindly and equally. They want to make sure that everyone is safe and that such sad things don't happen again. They want to remember the people who got hurt and make sure that their voices are heard so that Kenya can be a place where everyone feels safe to live and share their ideas without fear. They believe that everyone deserves to be protected.

Who Are "Gen Z"?

"Gen Z" is just a fun name for people who were born roughly between the late 1990s and the early 2010s. So, they are probably in their teens and early twenties now. They are very clever and often use the internet and social media to share their ideas and talk to each other. They are sometimes called 'digital natives' because they grew up with computers and phones all around them! These young people are showing that they care deeply about their future and the future of their country. They are learning how to speak up and stand together for what they believe is right. It's like when you and your friends team up to make sure everyone follows the rules of a game, so it's fair for all. Did you know? Young people all over the world are sometimes called 'Gen Z', not just in Kenya! They are known for being very passionate about important issues like climate change and fairness for everyone.

What Do Police Do?

Police officers are important people in our communities. Their job is to help everyone follow rules and stay safe. They wear uniforms and drive special cars, and they are there to help when there's a problem or when people need help. In Nairobi, the police are helping to manage the gathering of young people. By sealing off or closing some roads, they are trying to make sure that the people who are gathering can do so safely, and that other people in the city also stay safe. It's their way of keeping order and preventing accidents or problems when many people are in one place. They want to make sure that everyone can express their feelings in a peaceful way. Did you know? In Kenya, like many other African countries, police also help protect wild animals in national parks from harm, which is a very important job!

What Is "Justice" for Kids?

For you, 'justice' might mean that if someone takes your toy without asking, they give it back and say sorry. Or if someone breaks a rule in a game, they have to fix it so the game is fair again. For the young people in Nairobi, justice means that when sad things happen, like people getting hurt or losing their lives, the grown-ups in charge should try their best to understand why it happened and make sure it doesn't happen again. They want to make sure that the people who were hurt are remembered and that everyone feels safe and valued in their community. It's about ensuring fairness and safety for every single person in Kenya. They want to live in a country where everyone feels protected and cared for.

Why Is This Important for All of Us?

It's very important that people can share their feelings and ideas, especially if they think something isn't fair or could be better. When young people speak up, it shows that they care about their country and its future. It's a way for them to help make their community and country a better place for everyone, including you! We all want to live in a peaceful and safe world where everyone is treated kindly and fairly. When people talk about problems and work together, it helps us find solutions. We hope that the young people in Nairobi can share their message safely and that their voices will help make Kenya an even more wonderful place for all its children and grown-ups. Did you know? Kenya is also home to Mount Kenya, the second-highest mountain in Africa! It's a beautiful country with lots of amazing things to see and learn about.
summary: Young people in Nairobi, Kenya, called 'Gen Z,' are gathering to ask for justice and safety after sad events where people were hurt, and police are closing roads to help keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: It's important because young people are speaking up for fairness and safety, showing that everyone has a voice to help make their country a better place for all, and we hope for peace for everyone.

originalTitle: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
title: A Sad Story and Big Lessons About Being Safe at School
content:
Hello everyone! Your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa is here. Today, we have some very important, but also a little bit sad, news to share. It's about a school in Kenya, a beautiful country in East Africa, where something difficult happened. We need to talk about it so we can all learn how to stay safe.

A Sad Day at Utumishi School

Not long ago, there was a very, very sad event at a place called Utumishi Girls' School in Kenya. This is a school where girls go to learn and grow, just like you. But one night, something scary happened. There was a fire in one of the big rooms where the girls sleep. This special room is called a dormitory. Imagine you are at school, and you are sleeping in a big room with all your friends. It's usually a very safe and happy place. But this time, a fire started. Fires can be very dangerous and scary. They can make lots of smoke and get very hot. Sadly, because of this fire, sixteen of the older girls from the school did not wake up. These girls were between 15 and 18 years old. That's like your big brothers or big sisters, or perhaps your older cousins. It made many, many people in Kenya and all around the world very sad. Everyone felt a big sadness in their hearts for these girls and their families.

What is a Dormitory?

For many children in Africa, and in other places too, school is not just a place they go to during the day. Sometimes, school is far from home. So, children live at school in special buildings. These buildings have dormitories, which are like big bedrooms where many children sleep together. It's like having a big sleepover with all your friends every night! You eat together, play together, and sleep in the same building. It can be a lot of fun, making new friends and learning to be grown-up. But because so many children are together, it's super important for these places to be very, very safe. Did you know? Many schools in Kenya and other African countries are boarding schools, meaning students live there. This helps children who live far away get a good education.

Why is Safety So Important?

Safety is super important everywhere we go, right? At home, at the playground, and especially at school. Our teachers and parents always tell us to be careful. They want us to be safe from harm. When many children are living together in a dormitory, there are special rules to keep everyone safe. Things like knowing where the fire exits are, having fire alarms that make a loud noise if there's smoke, and learning what to do in an emergency are very, very important. We practice fire drills so we know how to leave a building quickly and safely if there's ever a fire. These rules help everyone stay calm and safe. Did you know? Fire drills help us practice what to do if there's a fire. It's like practicing a dance so you know all the steps!

Finding Out What Happened

When something very sad happens, like this fire, grown-ups need to find out why. They ask lots of questions. Was it an accident? Did someone do something wrong by mistake? Or did someone do something bad on purpose? Finding answers helps everyone understand and also helps to make sure that a sad thing like this never, ever happens again. In Kenya, grown-ups called investigators are working very hard to find out exactly how the fire started. They are talking to many people, like teachers, other students, and school workers. They want to put all the pieces of the puzzle together. Sometimes, when adults find out that someone did something very wrong that hurt others, they might ask them to go to a special court. This is so that everyone learns that we must always be careful and kind, and never do things that can hurt others. For this fire, some of the older students at the school are being asked many questions. This is because the grown-ups want to know everything that happened that night, so they can understand and help prevent future sad events. It's a very serious process, but it's all about trying to keep everyone safe in the future. Did you know? Police officers and special investigators are like detectives who try to solve mysteries to keep people safe.

Coming Together as a Community

When sad things happen, people often come together to help each other. This is called being a community. In Kenya, and everywhere, people felt very sad for the families of the girls who died. They prayed for them, sent messages of comfort, and tried to help in any way they could. Teachers, parents, and neighbours all supported each other. It shows how important it is to be kind and caring, especially when someone is hurting. We learn that even when something very sad happens, we can find strength by helping each other and showing love. Did you know? Many people in Kenya wore special ribbons or held candles to remember the girls who were lost.

Lessons for All of Us

Even though this is a very sad story, it teaches us some very important lessons. It teaches us to always be careful, to listen to our teachers about safety rules, and to look out for our friends. It reminds us that our schools should always be safe and happy places where we can learn and play without worry. Let's all remember to be safe, listen to rules, and be kind to everyone. Because when we are careful and look out for each other, we make our schools and our communities stronger and safer places for everyone. We hope that something like this never happens again, and that all children can feel safe and happy at school.
summary: Sixteen older girls tragically died in a dormitory fire at Utumishi Girls' School in Kenya, prompting grown-ups to investigate what happened to ensure all schools are safe and to learn important lessons about fire safety.
whyMatters: This is important because it reminds us how crucial it is to keep all children safe at school, especially where they sleep, and it teaches us to always follow safety rules and look out for each other so sad things like this don't happen again.

originalTitle: Sexual violence increasingly used as 'weapon of war' in Sudan, UN says
title: Big Helpers for Our Friends in Sudan!
content:
Hello, My Amazing Friends!

Hello, super kids of Africa and beyond! It’s your friend reporter here, bringing you some important news from our wonderful continent. Today, we're talking about our neighbours and friends in a country called Sudan. Sudan is a very big country, not too far from some of our homes, right here in Africa! It’s a place with beautiful deserts, mighty rivers, and lots of wonderful people, just like you and me. But right now, some of our friends in Sudan need a bit of extra help. They are going through a tough time, and it's important that we understand and send them our good thoughts and hopes for peace. Did you know? Sudan is the third-largest country in Africa! That's a lot of land and a lot of amazing people.

What is Happening in Sudan?

Imagine if some grown-ups in your town started having a big disagreement. Not just talking, but actual fighting. This is what has been happening in parts of Sudan. When grown-ups fight, it can make everyone feel very, very scared. It makes places that used to be safe, feel unsafe. Sometimes, when people are fighting, they do things that are not kind at all. They might try to scare other people, not just with big noises, but with mean actions. This is very unfair and makes mummies, daddies, and especially children, feel very unsafe and worried. It’s like if a bully in the playground didn’t just push, but also tried to make you feel very, very small and scared on purpose. But this is happening to many, many people in Sudan. It is not fair, and it is not right.

Why Are People So Scared?

The people who are fighting are doing very bad things. They are trying to make whole communities, which means groups of families living together, feel very, very scared. They do these mean things so that people will be afraid and maybe even leave their homes. Imagine having to leave your warm bed and all your toys because you are too scared to stay. That is what some children in Sudan are facing. These bad actions are making people feel very, very unsafe. It’s like they are trying to hurt people’s hearts and make them feel like they have no protection. This kind of hurting is very serious, and it makes people feel very sad and worried about their future. Did you know? Sometimes, when people are fighting, they forget that everyone deserves to feel safe and loved, no matter what.

The United Nations (UN) to the Rescue!

But guess what? There are amazing helpers in the world! A very special group called the United Nations, or UN for short, is working hard to help our friends in Sudan. The UN is like a super big team of helpers from almost every country in the world! They work together to solve big problems and help people who are in trouble. The UN has many kind people who are like superheroes, but instead of capes, they wear uniforms and have big hearts. They fly all over the world to help children and families. They want to make sure everyone is safe and happy. Did you know? The UN was created many years ago to help countries talk to each other and stop big fights, and to help people all over the world!

How is the UN Helping Our Friends?

The kind people from the UN are doing many important things in Sudan. First, they are telling everyone, especially the grown-ups who are fighting, that these mean actions must stop. They are saying, “Hey! This is not fair! You cannot make people feel so scared and hurt!” They speak very loudly for the people who are suffering. They also help by giving food, clean water, and medicine to families who have had to leave their homes. They set up safe places where children can play and learn, even if it's far from their old homes. They are like very kind teachers and doctors, making sure everyone is looked after. The UN is also trying to help make new rules that will stop these bad things from ever happening again. They want to protect children and families so they can live in peace and grow up strong and happy. Did you know? Giving someone clean water can save their life! The UN helps make sure people have the most important things to stay healthy.

Why is This So Important for All of Us?

This news is important because every child, everywhere in the world, deserves to feel safe. Every mummy, every daddy, every grandma and grandpa, deserves to feel safe and happy in their home. When something bad happens to our friends in Sudan, it makes us all think about how important peace is. We all want our friends in Africa to live happy lives, where they can go to school, play with their friends, and dream big dreams without any fear. The UN is helping to make sure that these dreams can come true for the children of Sudan. It’s a reminder that we should always be kind to each other and try to solve our problems by talking, not by fighting or by making others feel scared. Did you know? When you are kind to your friends and share your toys, you are helping to make peace in your own small world!

Wishing for Peace in Sudan

So, my lovely friends, let's all send our warmest wishes and hopes for peace to the children and families in Sudan. Let's hope that the fighting stops soon, and that everyone can go back to living safe, happy lives. We know the UN and many good people are working very hard to make this happen. Let's remember that even though bad things sometimes happen, there are always good people trying to make things better. And we can all be part of that goodness by being kind, sharing, and helping others around us. Stay safe, stay kind, and remember that we are all one big family here in Africa and across the world! We are strong together!
summary: Helpers from all over the world, like the UN, are working hard to keep children and families safe in Sudan, where some grown-ups are fighting and doing mean things that make people feel very scared and unsafe.
whyMatters: It's important because every child and every person deserves to feel safe and happy, and we want all our friends in Africa to live in peace, learn, and grow up without fear.

originalTitle: South Africa celebrates reaching World Cup knockout stage for first time
title: South Africa's Soccer Team Makes History at the World Cup!
content:
South Africa's Big Soccer Win!

This is such exciting news for everyone in Africa! Our friends in South Africa are jumping for joy! Their amazing soccer team has done something super special. They have reached a very big part of the World Cup tournament for the very first time! This is like winning a gold star for excellent work at school, but for a whole country!

What is the World Cup?

Imagine a giant party where all the best soccer teams from all over the world come to play. That's kind of what the World Cup is! It's a huge competition where countries send their very best players to kick a ball and score goals. They want to find out which country has the absolute best soccer team in the whole wide world! Did you know? Soccer is also called football in many countries! It's the most popular sport on Earth!

Going to the 'Knockout Stage'

So, what does it mean to reach the "knockout stage"? Think of it like a game of musical chairs, but with soccer teams! At first, many teams play in groups. Some teams win their games, and some teams don't. The teams that play really, really well get to move on to the next round. This next round is called the "knockout stage." In the knockout stage, if you lose a game, you are "knocked out" of the competition, and your World Cup adventure is over. But if you win, you keep going to play another game! It's very exciting because every game is super important. Did you know? In a regular soccer game, each team has 11 players on the field trying to score goals!

How South Africa Made It Happen

South Africa's team played a super important game recently. They played against another team from a country called South Korea. Our South African players ran very fast, kicked the ball very well, and worked together as a team. They tried their very best to score goals, and they also tried hard to stop South Korea from scoring any goals themselves. And guess what? South Africa's team won the game! Yes! They beat South Korea! This win was super important because it meant they could keep playing in the World Cup. It meant they got to move to the special knockout stage!

A First for South Africa!

Why is this news so special? Because it has never, ever happened before! South Africa's team has played in the World Cup before, but they have never gotten this far. This is their first time reaching the knockout stage. It's like being the first person in your family to climb a very tall mountain! Everyone is so proud. Did you know? The World Cup happens only every four years! It's a long wait for such an exciting event, which makes it even more special when a team does well!

Celebrating a Big Win

When the game ended, and South Africa knew they had won, everyone was so happy! The players on the field jumped up and down. They hugged each other tightly. People watching at home and in the stadiums cheered very loudly. There was singing and dancing everywhere! It was a big party to celebrate this amazing achievement. This win brings so much happiness and pride to all the people in South Africa. It shows that hard work and working together as a team can help you reach your biggest dreams. Many children in South Africa look up to these players. They might even dream of playing for their country one day!

What Happens Next?

Now that South Africa's team is in the knockout stage, they will play even more exciting games. They will play against another very strong team from a different country. They will keep trying their best to win and go even further in the competition. Every game will be a new adventure, and we will all be cheering for them! Did you know? A soccer goal is worth one point! The team with more points at the end of the game wins the match!

Dreams Come True

This story is a wonderful example for all of us. It tells us that if you keep trying, if you work hard, and if you never give up, you can achieve amazing things. South Africa's team never gave up on their dream. And now, that dream is coming true on a very big stage! Everyone in Africa is cheering for South Africa's team. We wish them good luck for their next games! Go, South Africa, go! You make us all very proud! Did you know? South Africa is a country in the southern part of Africa! Its flag has many bright colours: black, green, yellow, blue, white, and red, which stand for different things about their country and its people!

Being a Team Player

Playing soccer is not just about kicking a ball. It's about working together. Each player has a special job on the field. Some players protect their goal, some players pass the ball very carefully, and some players try to score. When everyone works together like a big family, they can achieve great things, just like South Africa's team did! This big achievement will inspire many young boys and girls in South Africa and all over Africa. They will see that with dedication, practice, and a good heart, anything is possible. It’s not just a game; it's a powerful lesson in never giving up and always hoping for the best. Did you know? The very first World Cup was held way back in 1930! That's a very, very long time ago, even before your grandparents were born!
summary: South Africa's soccer team has made history by reaching the World Cup knockout stage for the first time ever after beating South Korea, making everyone in their country very proud and happy.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that hard work and teamwork can help you reach big dreams, and it inspires all the children in South Africa to believe in themselves and their country's amazing athletes.

originalTitle: ICC judges sue Trump over sanctions, call measures unlawful
title: World Court Judges Ask for Help Against a Former Leader!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's your favorite kid-friendly news reporter, here to share a big story from our wonderful continent of Africa and beyond! Today, we’re going to talk about something important that happened with some very special judges from a very special court. Don't worry, we'll keep it super simple and fun, just for you!

What is a "World Court"?

Imagine you and your friends are playing a game. Sometimes, things might go wrong, right? Maybe someone breaks a rule, or there's a big disagreement. When that happens, you might need a grown-up, like a teacher, to help decide what’s fair. Well, there's a huge, grown-up version of that for the whole world! It's called the International Criminal Court, or ICC for short. You can think of it as a 'World Court'. This court is a very important place where special judges work. Their job is to look into very, very serious problems that happen in the world, like when people hurt others very badly during a war. They try to make sure that people who do truly awful things are held responsible. Did you know? The ICC is in a country called the Netherlands. It's far away, but its work helps people all over the world, even right here in Africa!

Meet the Judges

So, at this special World Court, there are very clever and kind judges. These judges are like the referees in a big football match, but for really serious world problems. They listen to all sides of a story and try to make fair decisions. They want to make sure justice is done, which means making things right when something bad has happened. Their job is super important because they protect people who might be in danger. They work hard to stop very bad things from happening again in the future. They are impartial, which means they don't pick sides; they just follow the rules and evidence.

What Happened with a Former Leader?

Now, a little while ago, there was a man named Mr. Trump. He used to be the leader of a big country called America. Sometimes, leaders make big decisions, and sometimes those decisions can cause problems for others. Mr. Trump made a decision that upset the judges at the World Court. He put something called 'sanctions' on them. What are sanctions? Imagine if someone said, 'You can't use your pocket money anymore!' or 'You can't travel to fun places!' That's a bit like what sanctions are. It means he made it hard for the judges to travel and to use their money. He did this because he didn't like some of the things the World Court was looking into. He wanted them to stop investigating certain problems that involved his country, America, and another country called Israel. Did you know? Sanctions are often used by countries to try and get other countries or people to change their minds about something. But the judges felt this was unfair.

Why Were the Judges Unhappy?

The judges at the World Court felt very sad and upset about Mr. Trump's actions. They said that these sanctions were 'unlawful'. This means they thought what he did was against the rules and not fair. They felt like Mr. Trump was trying to 'punish' them and 'coerce' them. 'Punish' means to make someone suffer for something. And 'coerce' means to try and force someone to do what you want, even if they don't think it's right. It was like trying to scare them into changing their minds. The judges believe that everyone, no matter how powerful, should follow the rules of the World Court. They think it's very important for them to be able to do their job fairly, without anyone trying to stop them or tell them what to do.

What Does "Suing" Mean?

Because the judges were so unhappy, they decided to 'sue' Mr. Trump. What does it mean to 'sue'? It means they went to *another* court, a different one, and asked for help! They said, 'This is not fair, please make Mr. Trump take back his sanctions!' It's like when you have a problem with a friend, and you ask an older sibling or a parent to help you sort it out. The judges asked a different court to look at the rules and decide if Mr. Trump's actions were truly against the law. They wanted to show that even powerful leaders cannot just stop important courts from doing their job. They wanted to protect the World Court so it can continue to help people who have been hurt by very serious crimes, like war crimes.

Why This is Important for Us

This story is important for all of us, especially here in Africa. Many times, the World Court helps people in different parts of Africa who have suffered because of big problems. If someone can stop the World Court from doing its job, then who will stand up for people who are hurt? The judges are fighting to make sure that the World Court stays strong and fair for everyone, everywhere. It means that people who do very bad things, even powerful people, can still be looked at by the World Court. This gives hope to many families and communities that need justice. Did you know? Many African countries are part of the International Criminal Court, which means they believe in its work to help make the world a safer place!

Let's Remember

So, remember, even when big problems happen, there are brave people like these judges who work to make things right. They remind us that rules are important, and everyone should follow them. It's about fairness, justice, and making sure our world is a better place for all kids, just like you! Keep listening to our news for more amazing stories. Until next time, stay curious and kind!
summary: Judges from the International Criminal Court, a special world court, asked another court to help them after a former leader named Mr. Trump put special rules, called sanctions, on them because he didn't like their investigations into very serious crimes.
whyMatters: This matters because it shows that even powerful leaders should follow the rules, and it helps make sure the special World Court can keep doing its important job of helping people who have been hurt by very bad things, making the world fairer for everyone.

originalTitle: ‘We hugged each other and ran’: Venezuelans recount earthquake horror
title: ‘We hugged each other and ran’: Venezuelans recount earthquake horror
content: Survivors describe panic and collapsing buildings during two strong quakes as thousands of people feared dead.

originalTitle: 'I thought building would fall on top of me' - Venezuelans describe earthquake panic
title: Big Shakes Far, Far Away!
content:
Hello, My Little Friends!

Hello, hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here. Today, we are talking about something that happened very far away, in a country called Venezuela. It's across the big ocean, on another continent. Even though it's far, it's good to know about our friends around the world. We will learn about big shakes in the ground! Sometimes, the ground we walk on can move and shake. It's called an earthquake. It can be a little scary when it happens. But don't worry, we will learn about it together in a very simple way.

What Happened Far, Far Away?

In Venezuela, a big city called the capital had a surprise. The ground started to shake! Imagine your favourite toy car shaking all by itself. That's a bit like what happened, but much, much bigger! The earth shook not once, but two times! It happened very quickly, one after the other. Boom! Then another boom! People felt the ground wobble and sway. This was a very, very strong shake. Did you know? Venezuela is a beautiful country with beaches and mountains. It's a long, long way from where we are in Africa.

The Earth Began to Shake!

Scientists have a way to measure how strong an earthquake is. It's like measuring how tall you are, but for how much the ground shakes. They use something called 'magnitude'. The first shake was a 'magnitude 7.2'. That's like a very, very big thump! The second shake was even stronger, 'magnitude 7.5'. This means it was super, super powerful! When the ground shakes so much, big buildings can wobble. Trees can sway. Things on shelves can fall down. It can be very surprising for everyone.

"Oh No, The Building Might Fall!"

Imagine you are inside your house, playing with your toys. Suddenly, everything starts to shake! What would you do? Many people in Venezuela felt very scared. One person said, "I thought the building would fall on top of me!" Can you imagine feeling that way? It means they felt really, really worried. People quickly ran outside to be safe. They wanted to be in an open space, away from tall buildings that might wobble. It's like when you play hide-and-seek, and you quickly run to your safe spot. For these people, outside was their safe spot away from the shaking buildings. They just wanted to be safe and sound.

Did You Know? What is an Earthquake?

Our Earth has a skin, just like an orange! But the Earth's skin is made of giant puzzle pieces. We call these 'plates'. These plates are always, always moving very, very slowly. Sometimes, these big puzzle pieces bump into each other. Or they slide past each other. When they do, they can get stuck for a little while. Then, suddenly, they slip! When they slip, it makes the ground shake. That shaking is what we call an earthquake. It's the Earth stretching and moving its big, big skin! Did you know? Most earthquakes happen near the edges of these big Earth plates. It's like where the puzzle pieces meet!

Did You Know? How Strong Was It?

A magnitude 7.2 or 7.5 earthquake is very, very strong. Think about a small shake from your little brother or sister. That's a tiny shake. Then think about a giant jumping up and down! That's how strong a magnitude 7.5 earthquake can feel. These strong shakes can make things move a lot. They can break windows or make cracks in walls. Luckily, many people in Venezuela were safe. But it was still a very scary time for them.

Keeping Safe During a Shake!

If you ever feel the ground shake, there are three important things to remember. They are called 'Drop, Cover, and Hold On'! 1. **Drop!** Drop to the ground right away. Get on your hands and knees. This stops you from falling over. 2. **Cover!** Crawl under a strong table or desk. If there's no table, cover your head and neck with your arms. This protects you from things that might fall. 3. **Hold On!** Hold onto your table or desk. Stay there until the shaking stops. If it moves, move with it! It's very important to stay calm. Being calm helps you remember what to do to keep safe. Practice these steps with your family, just in case!

Did You Know? Animals Can Feel Shakes Too!

Sometimes, animals can feel an earthquake coming even before people do! Birds might fly away very fast. Dogs might start barking or acting strangely. Cats might hide. Animals are very clever! They can feel the tiny vibrations in the ground that we humans cannot feel. It's like they have super-sensors!

A Big Hug to Our Friends in Venezuela

So, my little friends, we learned about the big ground shakes in Venezuela. It was a scary time for the people there. But they were brave, and they helped each other. Even though we live far away in Africa, it's nice to send our good thoughts to everyone. We hope everyone there is now safe and sound. Remember to always be kind and help others, no matter where they are. Thank you for listening, and keep exploring the amazing world around us!
summary: People in Venezuela felt two very strong ground shakes, called earthquakes, which made buildings wobble and scared many, but they stayed safe by running outside.
whyMatters: It's important to learn about earthquakes so we know what they are, how strong they can be, and how to keep ourselves safe if the ground ever starts to shake.

originalTitle: France, UK and Spain see record temperatures as heatwave grips western Europe
title: Wow! Europe is Super Hot Right Now!
content:
Hello, Super Reporters!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter for Africa here, ready to share some big news from far away. Today, we're talking about something called a 'heatwave' happening in some countries far, far away, across the big ocean. It's making things super, super hot for lots of people! Let's find out more.

What is a Heatwave?

Imagine the sun shining extra, extra bright and hot for many days in a row. That's what a heatwave is! It means the air temperature gets much, much higher than it usually is. It's like the sun has decided to give everyone a really big, warm hug for too long! **Did you know?** A heatwave can make the roads feel like they are baking! Sometimes, it's even hot enough to fry an egg on the pavement, but please don't try that at home!

Where is it Super Hot?

This super hot weather is happening in some countries in a place called Europe. The countries we are talking about are France, the United Kingdom (or UK for short), and Spain. These places are very far from us in Africa, but they are feeling a lot of heat, just like we sometimes do here. Usually, these countries are not this hot. Sometimes they are quite cool. But right now, the sun is shining extra strong there. Many, many people are feeling the heat. It's making them sweat and feel a bit tired.

Why is it So Hot?

The temperatures in these countries are reaching 'record' levels. That means it's hotter than it has ever been before, for a very long time! Imagine a thermometer, that red line is going higher and higher than it has ever gone before in these places. Our Earth is like a big house, and sometimes, parts of our house get warmer than they used to. Scientists say that our Earth is getting a little bit warmer over many, many years. This can sometimes make heatwaves happen more often or make them even hotter. **Did you know?** The hottest place on Earth ever recorded was in Death Valley, USA. It reached a super scorching 56.7 degrees Celsius! That's almost like boiling water!

How Does This Heat Feel?

When it's super hot like this, people feel very sticky and sweaty. It can be hard to play outside for a long time. They might feel sleepy or not want to run around too much. It's important to be careful when it's this hot. Children and grown-ups are trying their best to stay cool. They are looking for shade, staying indoors, and drinking lots and lots of water. Some people are going to swimming pools to jump in the cool water!

Keeping Safe in the Sun

Even though this news is from far away, these tips are great for us here in Africa too, when it gets hot! When the sun is very strong, it's good to remember a few things: * **Drink lots of water!** Water is your best friend when it's hot. It helps your body stay cool and happy. Keep a bottle of water with you. * **Find some shade!** If you're outside, try to stay under a tree or an umbrella. The shade helps to keep you cool. * **Wear light clothes!** Choose clothes that are light in color and not too thick. White or light colours help reflect the sun's heat away. * **Stay inside during the hottest part of the day!** If you can, play inside when the sun is at its strongest, usually around lunchtime. * **Take cool baths or showers!** A quick splash in cool water can feel wonderful. **Did you know?** Your body has a special way to cool itself down! When you sweat, that sweat evaporates from your skin, and it helps to take some of the heat away with it. It's like your body's own air conditioner!

What Are Red Alerts?

When it gets super, super hot like it is now, the grown-ups who look after the weather send out special messages called 'red alerts'. Imagine a traffic light changing to red. It means: 'STOP! Be very, very careful!' These red alerts tell everyone that the heat is very dangerous. It means people should try extra hard to stay cool and safe. They tell doctors and hospitals to be ready in case anyone gets sick from the heat.

Helping Each Other

When it's this hot, people are also helping each other. Grown-ups are checking on older people or those who might need help. They make sure everyone has enough water and a cool place to stay. It's all about being kind and looking out for your friends and neighbours.

A Message From Your Reporter!

So, while it's super hot in Europe, it reminds us to always be careful when the sun is shining brightly, no matter where we are. Remember to drink your water and find your shade! Let's hope the weather cools down soon for our friends across the ocean. Stay safe and keep learning, my super reporters! Bye for now!
summary: Europe is experiencing a record-breaking heatwave with very hot temperatures in countries like France, UK, and Spain, causing 'red alerts' and making it important for everyone to stay cool and safe by drinking water and finding shade.
whyMatters: This is important because very hot weather, called a heatwave, can make people sick if they don't stay cool and drink enough water, and it shows how parts of our Earth are getting warmer, which can make things different for everyone.

originalTitle: Trump asks Congress for $87bn, mostly for 'urgent' Iran war costs
title: Big Money Talk Far Away!
content:
Hello, My Amazing Friends in Africa!

Hello everyone! It’s your friendly news reporter, here to share a story from far, far away. We’re going to talk about something important happening in a country called America. Sometimes, grown-ups talk about big numbers and big problems, and it can be a bit confusing. But don't worry, we'll make it super simple, like counting your fingers and toes! This story is about money, a lot of money, and what it might be used for. It's also about people talking and trying to agree, or sometimes, disagree.

A Big Request for Money

Imagine you have a piggy bank. Inside your piggy bank, you save money for something special, maybe a new toy or a yummy treat. Well, in America, there is a very big piggy bank for the whole country. A person named Mr. Trump, who used to be the boss of America, asked for a HUGE amount of money from this piggy bank. How much money? He asked for something called "87 billion dollars"! Wow! That’s a number so big, it’s hard to even imagine. It’s like having 87 with nine zeros after it! That’s more money than you could ever count in your whole life.

Who is Mr. Trump?

Mr. Trump used to be the President of America. The President is like the big leader of the country. He makes important decisions and helps guide the country. So, when Mr. Trump asked for this money, it was a very important request. He wanted the money for things he called "urgent war costs" with a country called Iran.

What is Congress?

Now, who gives the money from the big piggy bank? It’s not just one person. In America, there’s a group of people called "Congress." Congress is like a very, very big team of people. Their job is to help make rules for the country and decide how the country's money should be spent. Think of them as the guardians of the big piggy bank. When Mr. Trump asked for money, he had to ask Congress to give it to him. Congress has to agree first.

So Much Money!

So, Mr. Trump asked Congress for 87 billion dollars. He said he needed it very quickly, or "urgently," to help with things related to a possible fight with Iran. This money would be for soldiers, for special equipment, and for everything that happens when countries are not getting along. It’s a lot, lot, lot of money that could do so many other things. **Did you know?** If you had 87 billion dollars, you could build many, many new schools all across Africa! Or buy millions of books for children just like you! It’s enough money to help so many people.

Why Are People Unhappy?

Now, here’s where the story gets a little tricky. Mr. Trump asked for this money. But guess what? Many people in Congress were not very happy about it. Just one day before he asked for the money, these lawmakers (that’s what we call the people in Congress) told Mr. Trump off! They said they didn’t like some of the things he had already done with soldiers. They told him that his "military action" – which means what his soldiers did – was not good. They "rebuked" him, which is a grown-up word for saying, "We are really not happy with what you did!"

What is an "Uphill Battle"?

Because Congress was already unhappy, getting this new money was going to be very hard. The news story says it "faces an uphill battle." Imagine you are trying to climb a very, very steep hill. It’s tough, right? You have to push hard and work very, very much to get to the top. That’s what "an uphill battle" means here. It means it will be very, very difficult for Mr. Trump to get Congress to give him all that money for fighting. They might say no, or they might say, "Let’s talk about it for a very long time first."

Fighting Far Away (Iran)

The money Mr. Trump asked for was for something about a country called Iran. Iran is very far away from us, in a part of the world called Asia. When countries have problems, sometimes they get into fights. These fights are called wars. Wars are very sad because people get hurt, homes get broken, and lots of things get destroyed. Nobody wants wars, especially children who just want to play and learn. **Did you know?** Instead of fighting, countries can talk to each other to solve their problems. This is called diplomacy, and it means people sit down and chat to find a peaceful way forward. It's like when you have a disagreement with a friend, and you talk it out instead of pushing or shouting.

What Could All That Money Do?

Let’s think about that 87 billion dollars again. If it wasn't used for fighting, what amazing things could it do? It could help build hospitals to make sick people better. It could provide clean water for villages that don’t have it. It could help plant trees to make our Earth healthier. It could buy lots of food for children who are hungry. It could even help scientists find cures for illnesses. It makes you think, doesn't it? Such a huge amount of money could do so much good in the world!

Hoping for Peace

So, what does this all mean for us? Even though this is happening far away, it reminds us that peace is very important everywhere. We all want a world where people are kind to each other and solve problems by talking, not by fighting. It also reminds us that money is a powerful tool. It can be used for good things, like building and helping, or for sad things, like fighting. We can always hope that leaders around the world choose to use money and their power for peace and for making people's lives better. Let’s always wish for a peaceful world for everyone!
summary: Former U.S. President Mr. Trump asked for a huge amount of money, 87 billion dollars, for urgent costs related to a possible fight with a country called Iran, but Congress, who guards the country's money, was already unhappy with his actions and might make it very hard for him to get it.
whyMatters: This story shows that leaders have to ask for money, and others decide if it's a good idea, reminding us that it's always better to use money for peace and helping people rather than for fighting far away.

originalTitle: Vinicius Jr stops fun and leaves Scots down... but are they out?
title: Big Football Fun! Brazil vs. Scotland!
content:
Hello, amazing kids! Get ready for some super exciting news from the world of football! Football is a wonderful game where two teams try to kick a ball into a special net called a goal. It's played all over the world, and many kids just like you love to play it! Today, we're talking about a big game that happened far away, in a sunny place called Miami. It was a game between two strong and brave teams: Brazil and Scotland! Brazil is a country very famous for its amazing football players. They wear bright yellow and green shirts when they play! Scotland is another country, and their players wear blue shirts. Both teams love to play football, and they always try their very best to win. Let's find out what happened in their big game!

The Big Game Day!

Imagine a huge green field, bright lights shining down, and many, many people cheering very loudly! That's what it was like for this special football match. Both teams ran onto the field, feeling excited and ready to play their very best. They wanted to score lots of goals and make all their fans happy. Playing football is like a big, energetic dance with a ball. Players run very fast, pass the ball to their teammates, and try to trick the other team to get closer to the goal. It's all about working together as a team! Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in the whole world! Billions of people love to watch it, and even more love to play it. In our amazing continent of Africa, we love football too! We have many fantastic teams and players who make us proud.

Vinicius Jr. – A Super Player!

In the Brazil team, there is a very fast and clever player. His name is Vinicius Jr.! He is like a superhero with the ball when he plays! When Vinicius Jr. gets the ball, he can dribble past other players with incredible speed. Dribbling means running with the ball very close to your feet, like the ball is glued to you! It takes a lot of practice to do it so well. In this special game, Vinicius Jr. was super, super good. He helped his team, Brazil, to score important goals. When a team scores a goal, everyone cheers with joy, and the players jump up and down! It's like when you try really hard at school and do something amazing – everyone is proud of you! Vinicius Jr. made his team and his country very proud that day. He showed everyone how much he loves to play and how skilled he is. Did you know? Many famous African football players, like Sadio Mané from Senegal and Mohamed Salah from Egypt, are also super fast and score lots of goals, just like Vinicius Jr.! They are heroes to many kids in Africa!

Scotland Tries Their Best!

Now, what about the Scottish team? Did they give up because Brazil had a super player? No way! The Scottish team tried their very, very hardest. They ran, they kicked, they passed, and they worked together to defend their goal. Defending means stopping the other team from scoring. It's a very important job in football to keep the ball away from your own net! Sometimes, even when you try with all your heart and give 100%, things don't go exactly as you want. In this game, Brazil played a little better and managed to score more goals this time. So, Brazil won the game. This made the Scottish players feel a little sad, of course, because nobody likes to lose. But they showed great sportsmanship. Sportsmanship means being kind, fair, and respectful to the other team, whether you win or lose. It's all about playing fairly and having fun, and shaking hands after the game. Did you know? Even if a team loses a game, they can learn many important things from it! They can think about what they did well and what they can do even better next time. Every game is a chance to learn and become stronger for the future!

What's Next for Scotland? Are They Out?

You might be thinking, "Oh no, Scotland lost! Does that mean they can't play any more games?" Well, this game was part of a very big football party called a "tournament." A tournament is like many games played one after another, over many days or weeks, all leading to one big winner at the very end. Imagine you are playing a game with your friends. If you lose one round, it doesn't mean you stop playing forever, right? You might get another chance in the next round or the next game! For Scotland, even though they lost this one game to Brazil, they might still have a chance to keep playing in the big tournament. There are usually many teams in a tournament, and sometimes a team can lose one game but still play more games and do well later. It's like a big puzzle. You need to win enough games, or get enough points, to move on to the next level. Scotland still has a chance to solve their puzzle and keep playing! So, everyone is waiting to see what happens next for Scotland. Will they get to play in the "last 32" teams? That means being one of the best 32 teams left in the whole competition! We'll have to wait and see, but they still have hope. Did you know? In many big football tournaments, teams play in small groups first. If they do well enough in their group, they can still go forward in the tournament, even if they lose one game!

Fun and Friendship Through Football!

No matter who wins or loses, football is all about having fun and bringing people together! It brings people from all over the world closer. Players from different countries become friends, and fans cheer for their teams and often make new friends too. Football teaches us many important lessons that are useful in life: * **Teamwork:** Working together makes you strong and helps you achieve great things. * **Trying Your Best:** Always give 100% effort in everything you do! * **Never Give Up:** Even if it's hard, keep trying and learning! * **Sportsmanship:** Be kind, respectful, and fair to everyone. We are so lucky to have such an exciting game that brings joy and excitement to so many people around the world, especially here in Africa! So, let's keep watching the football games and cheer for all the players who work so hard and have so much fun! Did you know? When players shake hands and hug after a game, it shows respect and friendship, even after a tough match where they tried hard to beat each other! It's a beautiful part of sports.
summary: Brazil beat Scotland in a fun football game where Vinicius Jr. played very well, but Scotland still has a chance to play more games in the big tournament.
whyMatters: This game shows us how much fun football is, how important it is for all players to try their best, and how teams can still have a chance in a tournament even if they lose one game, teaching us about perseverance and good sportsmanship.

originalTitle: 'Think we're going home' - Clarke & McGinn rue Scotland mistakes
title: Big Sports Dreams and Waiting for News!
content:
Hello, Young Sports Fans!

Hello everyone! It’s your friendly news reporter for Africa here. Today, we want to talk about big sports games and trying your very best. Imagine playing your favorite game with your friends, like football or netball. You run fast, you kick the ball, you try to score goals! It's so exciting to play. Sometimes, teams from different countries play together in very, very big games. These games are watched by many people all around the world. It’s like a huge party where everyone celebrates sports!

What is a World Cup?

Have you ever heard of something called the World Cup? It’s one of the biggest sports events ever! Many teams, from many different countries, come together to play football. Each team wants to try and win the special trophy. It’s a very important competition. Did you know? The World Cup happens only once every four years! That means teams have a lot of time to practice and get ready. It's like waiting for a very special birthday party that only comes every four years.

Playing with All Your Heart

There was a team, let's call them Team Brave Hearts, who played in a big World Cup game recently. They ran, they kicked, they passed the ball with all their might. Every player on the team worked super hard. Their coach, like a teacher, told them what to do. They wanted to win so much, just like you want to win when you play a game. They trained for many, many days to be ready for these big matches. It takes a lot of practice to play well.

Waiting for Important News

After their game, Team Brave Hearts had to wait. Waiting can be a bit hard, can't it? Sometimes you wait for your food, or you wait for your turn to play. This team had to wait to find out if they could keep playing in the World Cup. It was like waiting for the results of a big test. Their coach and the players were thinking about it a lot. They wondered, "Will we go to the next round, or will our journey end?" Waiting for news like this can make your heart beat a little faster!

Sometimes We Make Mistakes

Even when you try your best, sometimes things don't go perfectly. Maybe you miss a shot in football, or you drop the ball in a game. It happens to everyone, even the best players in the world! Sometimes, teams make a few mistakes during a big game. These little errors can sometimes mean that the game doesn't go exactly as planned. But that's okay! Making mistakes is part of learning. We learn from them and try to do better next time. It's how we grow stronger and wiser, just like when you learn new things at school. Did you know? Even the greatest football players in the world make mistakes sometimes! But they always keep practicing and trying to get better. That's the spirit of a true champion!

What Does "Going Home" Mean?

The team's coach and players talked about "going home." This doesn't mean they forgot where their homes are! It means their journey in the World Cup might be over for now. If they "go home," it means they won't play any more games in *this* competition. One of the players, a very thoughtful person, thought it was "unlikely" they would stay. "Unlikely" means it probably won't happen. So, they thought they might have to go home soon. It's a bit sad when your adventure comes to an end, isn't it? But every journey has a start and an end.

Always Try Your Best!

Even if a team has to go home, it's important to remember how hard they tried. They gave their all for their country! They showed courage and worked together as a team. That’s what truly matters – the effort and the teamwork. It’s like when you try your best at school. Maybe you don’t get every answer right, but your teacher knows you worked hard. That effort is special. Every time a team plays, they learn something new. They learn how to play better, how to work together, and how to never give up. Did you know? In Africa, we love sports so much! Many children here dream of playing in big games like the World Cup one day. Keep practicing, keep dreaming, and always try your best!

A Message to All Our Young Athletes

So, what can we learn from this story? We learn that trying your best is always important. We learn that waiting for news can be hard, but we must be patient. And we learn that even if things don't go exactly as we hoped, there's always a next time. Keep playing your games, keep having fun, and always remember the joy of sports! Maybe one day, you will be the one playing in a big World Cup, making your country proud. Until then, keep shining bright!
summary: A team played very hard in a big World Cup game and is now waiting to find out if they can keep playing, but they think their journey might be over soon, reminding us that trying your best is always important.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us about trying our best in sports and in life, working together as a team, and being patient even when things don't go exactly as planned, which are important lessons for everyone.

originalTitle: What do Scotland need to make World Cup knockouts?
title: What do Scotland need to make World Cup knockouts?
content: After their 3-0 defeat by Brazil, BBC Sport looks at what Scotland require to reach the knockout phase.
